tool.name: Keep or remove selected individuals

param.name: vcf_input
param.label: VCF file
param.help: File containing SNP data in a VCF format. You can upload a simple file, multiple file or a dataset collection.

param.name: list_ind
param.label: List of individuals to be kept
param.help: Individuals must be listed one per line. If there are multiple VCF files, the individual list contain all individuals present in all VCF files.

tool.help:
Usage =====
As input, the user provides a single VCF file (Danecek *et al.* 2011) and a list of individuals (tabular or txt). The user selects the action they wish to perform: delete or keep only the individuals listed.
The tool processes the VCF file and writes an output VCF file in which it keeps or removes only the samples listed in the provided individual list. If any individual in the list is not present in the VCF, it is ignored, and processing continues with the available matches.
Example : The input VCF file contains individuals id_A, id_B, id_C, id_D and the list contains id_B, id_D and id_E. If the user decides to keep individuals, the output will contain only samples id_B and id_D. Samples id_A and id_C are removed and id_E is ignored. If the user decides to remove individuals, the output will contain only samples id_A and id_C.
Example of individual list :
::
id_B id_E id_D
The list of individuals does not contain a header and there is only one individual per line.
See also examples of VCF formats in : https://samtools.github.io/hts-specs/VCFv4.5.pdf

citations.citation: @article{10.1093/gigascience/giab008, author = {Danecek, Petr and Bonfield, James K and Liddle, Jennifer and Marshall, John and Ohan, Valeriu and Pollard, Martin O and Whitwham, Andrew and Keane, Thomas and McCarthy, Shane A and Davies, Robert M and Li, Heng}, title = "{Twelve years of SAMtools and BCFtools}", journal = {GigaScience}, volume = {10}, number = {2}, year = {2021}, abstract = "{SAMtools and BCFtools are widely used programs for processing and analysing high-throughput sequencing data. They include tools for file format conversion and manipulation, sorting, querying, statistics, variant calling, and effect analysis amongst other methods.The first version appeared online 12 years ago and has been maintained and further developed ever since, with many new features and improvements added over the years. The SAMtools and BCFtools packages represent a unique collection of tools that have been used in numerous other software projects and countless genomic pipelines.Both SAMtools and BCFtools are freely available on GitHub under the permissive MIT licence, free for both non-commercial and commercial use. Both packages have been installed \\>1 million times via Bioconda. The source code and documentation are available from https://www.htslib.org.}", issn = {2047-217X}, doi = {10.1093/gigascience/giab008}, url = {https://doi.org/10.1093/gigascience/giab008}, note = {giab008}, eprint = {https://academic.oup.com/gigascience/article-pdf/10/2/giab008/36332246/giab008.pdf}, }

@article{Danecek2011, author = {Danecek, Petr and Auton, Adam and Abecasis, Goncalo and Albers, Cornelis A. and Banks, Erica and DePristo, Mark A. and Handsaker, Robert E. and Lunter, Gerton and Marth, Gabor T. and Sherry, Stephen T. and McVean, Gil and Durbin, Richard and {1000 Genomes Project Analysis Group}}, title = {The variant call format and {VCF}tools}, journal = {Bioinformatics}, volume = {27}, number = {15}, pages = {2156-2158}, year = {2011}, doi = {10.1093/bioinformatics/btr330}, URL = {https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btr330}, eprint = {https://academic.oup.com/bioinformatics/article-pdf/27/15/2156/600788/btr330.pdf}, }
